title: lycopodiophyta
intro: The Lycopodiophyta division rank is a group of vascular plants that are characterized by their small, simple leaves and lack of seeds. They are non-vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Examples of divisions include Monocotyledonae, which encompasses plants with one cotyledon, such as grasses and lilies, Magnoliopsida, a division of flowering plants that includes trees and shrubs, and Polypodiopsida, a division of ferns. The Lycopodiophyta division specifically includes plants such as club mosses and spike mosses.

classes.class_desc: Lycopodiopsida is a class of ancient, non-vascular plants commonly known as club mosses.
Osmundopsida is an order of ancient, vascular plants commonly known as ferns of the whisk fern group.
